title: Coelogyne convallariiformis
taxonomy: Coelogyne convallariiformis is classified as a species of orchid in the genus Coelogyne. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Epidendroideae. This orchid species is native to the tropical regions of Asia. Within the Coelogyne genus, it is placed in the subgenus Coelogyne. The exact classification of Coelogyne convallariiformis is further detailed in botanical classifications.

intro: Coelogyne convallariiformis, also known as the Lily Orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne convallariiformis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
